collection_date: Over 12 million registered as of February 2025
Women hold 14% of mobile money accounts

original_text: First major mobile money service in Ethiopia
Historic: digital > cash for first time
Across the surveyed African countries, Ethiopia had the highest gender gaps in mobile Internet adoption (36%), smartphone ownership (34%) and overall mobile ownership (22%).
notes: Ethiopia has the widest smartphone ownership gender gap among GSMA-surveyed African countries; relevant enabler/proxy for Access forecasting per Enrichment Guide Sheet C.

original_text: Ethiopia's internet penetration rate stood at 21.7 percent of the total population at the end of the year, with 29.5 million internet users as of October 2025.
notes: Indirect/enabler indicator for digital payment usage per Enrichment Guide Sheet C (mobile internet access is a precondition for app-based digital payments).
Mobile money launches associated with medium-term account ownership gains per Suri & Jack (2016) Kenya M-Pesa evidence.
Direct: Telebirr is the primary driver of Ethiopia mobile money account growth (4.7% in 2021 to 9.45% in 2024).
Telebirr P2P transfers are the dominant use case per Enrichment Guide Sheet D (P2P dominance).
New MNO market entry historically increases mobile subscription penetration via competition/pricing effects.
M-Pesa entry directly contributed to mobile money account growth alongside Telebirr.
Direct causal link: registered users measured from this launch.
Direct: enrollment is a direct output of program rollout.
Digital ID reduces KYC friction for account opening; long lag reflects gradual integration into financial institution onboarding, per India Aadhaar literature.
FX liberalization is a macro shock; near-term effect on account ownership is ambiguous (inflation may deter saving, but banking system stabilization may encourage formal account use).
Milestone reflects concurrent substitution away from ATM cash withdrawal toward P2P digital transfer, not a forward-looking driver.
Interoperability between M-Pesa and EthSwitch network expected to increase cross-platform P2P transaction volume.
National instant payment rails (cf. Brazil Pix, India UPI) are associated with subsequent account ownership growth as merchants/consumers open accounts to access the rail.

NFIS-II sets the 70% account ownership target and coordinates the policy environment (agent licensing, interoperability mandates) that enables other events in this list; long lag reflects multi-year strategy implementation.
Price increases are expected to modestly dampen new subscription growth due to price elasticity of demand.
